informal: Prove that there are infinitely many primes congruent to $-1$ (modulo $4$).
lean:
theorem Artin_exercise_11_13_3 (N : ℕ):
  ∃ p ≥ N, Nat.Prime p ∧ p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]
reasoning: The informal claim asks for infinitely many primes p with p ≡ -1 (mod 4). The Lean statement says: for every natural number N, there exists p ≥ N such that p is prime and p+1 ≡ 0 (mod 4). The condition p+1 ≡ 0 (mod 4) is exactly p ≡ -1 (mod 4) (equivalently p ≡ 3 (mod 4)). The "for all N, exists p ≥ N" formulation is the standard Lean/Mathlib way of stating that there are infinitely many such primes, and it is logically equivalent to the informal claim. No extra hypotheses are imposed and nothing is dropped.
